title: Partial-win replay control

queries: ml partial win replay control rerun protocol variance candidate confirmation
deep learning candidate replay reproducibility structured experiment control

papers.title: N/A
papers.claim: This is a control protocol candidate rather than a literature-derived modeling idea.
papers.relevance: Used to test whether a recent partial-win bundle survives rerun variance once the baseline noise floor is known.

evidence.why_now: EXP087 showed that the unchanged refreshed baseline can still produce a strong user-ltv regression and an item-incoterms MRR drop almost identical to EXP078. That makes EXP078 the right next replay target: it was the strongest recent partial win, and its apparent salt-side failure may be partially confounded by baseline rerun variance.

hypothesis: Replaying the EXP078 split local/global graph-token packaging bundle under the same protocol will show whether its Amazon-side gains and salt-side regression are stable relative to the baseline rerun spread revealed by EXP087. This is a confirmation control, not a new modeling idea.
notes: Control only. Do not launch until the baseline noise-floor control has been summarized.
This bundle replays EXP078 exactly at the modeling level: split local/global graph-token packaging only.
Interpretation target: decide whether EXP078's user-ltv gain and item-incoterms regression are outside the baseline rerun spread.
